title: Deliciously Dehydrated Moroccan Peanut Tagine
description: A savory and kid-friendly Moroccan dish featuring peanut cookies in a glassy-jelly sauce, made in a traditional cazuela vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the crushed peanut cookies, cumin, coriander, and ginger.
In a large cazuela, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic to the cazuela and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the chicken breast to the cazuela and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the peanut cookie mixture, honey, and chicken broth to the cazuela and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the cazuela and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 hours, or until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the cazuela from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Use a dehydrator or your oven on the lowest temperature setting to dehydrate the tagine for 1 hour, or until the sauce has reached a glassy-jelly consistency.
